seoTitle: Sync a Power BI Slicer Across Multiple Report Pages
metaDescription: Learn how to sync a slicer across multiple pages in Power BI so one filter applies everywhere, without recreating slicers on every page.
intro: Rebuilding the same slicer on every report page wastes time and risks filters getting out of sync. This guide shows how to sync a single slicer across multiple pages in Power BI so one selection filters your entire report. It's aimed at anyone building multi-page dashboards for a team that expects consistent filtering.

steps.heading: Set up your slicers
steps.body: Add the slicer to one page first and confirm it's filtering the correct field before extending it to other pages.

steps.heading: Configure sync filters correctly
steps.body: Open the Sync Slicers pane, select the pages where you want the slicer to apply, and choose whether it should also be visible on each page or just apply its filter.

steps.heading: Understand the value of a unified dashboard
steps.body: A synced slicer keeps every page showing consistent, filtered data, which avoids confusion when stakeholders click between pages.

steps.heading: Apply automation techniques for reporting
steps.body: Once synced, the slicer's filter state carries across pages automatically, reducing the manual setup needed for future report updates.

steps.heading: Follow best practices for data visualization
steps.body: Keep synced slicers visually consistent in placement and style across pages so users always know where to find the active filter.

faq.question: Where do I find the Sync Slicers option in Power BI?
faq.answer: It's available under the View tab in Power BI Desktop — enabling it opens a pane where you can control which pages a slicer syncs to and whether it's visible on each.

faq.question: Can I sync a slicer's filter without showing it on every page?
faq.answer: Yes, the Sync Slicers pane lets you apply the filter to a page without displaying the slicer visual itself, which is useful for cleaner page layouts.

faq.question: Does syncing slicers work across different visual types?
faq.answer: Syncing applies to the filter behavior of the slicer itself, so as long as the target pages use fields connected to the synced field, the filter will apply consistently.

faq.question: Will a synced slicer slow down my report?
faq.answer: Synced slicers don't add significant overhead on their own, though performance still depends on the complexity of the visuals being filtered on each page.
